title: Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules
summary: A clear safety boundary for using the medical section during collapse, isolation, or no-ambulance conditions.
content: Place this warning before any extreme medical entry. The database can help organize first response, records, hygiene, transport, and escalation, but it cannot examine a person, diagnose illness, prescribe medicine, or replace a clinician. In worst-case conditions, the safest goal is to reduce immediate harm, avoid contamination, record changes, preserve dignity, and reach trained help as early as possible.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”.
paper and pen. Context: “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules»
steps: Read this before acting on any medical entry.
Use the guide to organize safety, hygiene, records, comfort, and transport.
Treat breathing trouble, heavy bleeding, unconsciousness, poisoning, severe burns, pregnancy danger signs, infants in distress, or fast worsening as urgent.
Do not improvise surgery, diagnosis, prescription changes, or invasive procedures.
Write who did what, when, and why so a professional can review later.
warnings: This is not medical advice and it is not a doctor's advice. It is offline emergency organization guidance only. Use local emergency numbers, trained first aid, pharmacists, clinics, doctors, poison centers, and official public-health instructions whenever available. Do not perform surgery, invasive procedures, diagnosis, prescription changes, or medicine dosing from this guide. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”.

Escalate immediately for trouble breathing, heavy bleeding, unconsciousness, severe pain, chest pain, stroke signs, severe burns, pregnancy danger signs, infants in distress, poisoning, or rapid worsening. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”.
Do not delay evacuation or professional care to finish a checklist. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”.
Keep privacy, consent, dignity, and local law in mind even during collapse. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”.
common_mistakes: Trying to diagnose instead of recognizing danger signs while carrying out “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”
Forgetting times, medicines, allergies, and changes while carrying out “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”
Touching fluids or wounds without hand hygiene while carrying out “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”
Moving an injured person without a safety reason while carrying out “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”
Letting panic, crowds, or rumors control care while carrying out “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”
alternatives: Use certified first-aid training where available as a fallback for “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”
Use official emergency services or the nearest clinic as a fallback for “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”
Use a pharmacist for medicine questions as a fallback for “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”
Use radio/phone consultation if transport is delayed as a fallback for “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”
Use written handover notes when devices fail as a fallback for “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”
failure_signs: Breathing, bleeding, consciousness, pain, fever, confusion, weakness, or skin color gets worse during “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”
The helper cannot keep the scene clean or safe during “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”
Transport, water, power, or communication failure blocks care during “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”
The person becomes unable to drink, speak, walk, recognize people, or stay awake during “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules”
when_not_to_use: Do not use it to replace a doctor, ambulance, clinic, pharmacist, poison center, or trained first-aider when using the “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules” record
Do not use it for surgery or invasive treatment when using the “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules” record
Do not use it to change prescription medicines or doses when using the “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules” record
Do not force care on a conscious adult except immediate life-safety actions allowed by local rules when using the “Extreme medical disclaimer and red-line rules” record
short_term: Reduce immediate danger, call or send for qualified help, keep the person clean, warm or cool as needed, and record facts without guessing.
long_term: Build certified first-aid capacity, update medical records, protect supplies, practice handovers, and map routes to pharmacies, clinics, hospitals, and shelters.
if_method_fails: Escalate by the fastest safe route: emergency number, clinic, hospital, pharmacist, poison center, radio medical consult, trained first-aider, or evacuation convoy. Record what failed and when.
environment_notes: In apartments, shelters, rubble zones, heatwaves, floods, smoke, and outages, medical problems worsen because of crowding, poor air, poor hygiene, no elevator, no refrigeration, and delayed transport.
